course_description: This course focuses on engagement, collaboration and the development of community partnerships. In this course, students gain the necessary skills to develop collaborative professional partnerships with communities as clients of speech pathology services.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
A detailed course outline for SPCH3211 is provided on the SPCH3211 Blackboard site. Please note that students are expected to attend all tutorials.
course_aims:
Evolving community expectations and needs have been drivers for change in the scope of practice for speech pathologists. Speech pathologists now practise in a wide range of contexts and with diverse people and communities. In this course, students will gain understanding of how to partner and collaborate with a community to identify the communication and swallowing needs and to advocate for communities in speech pathology practice. Through authentic case-based learning, students will develop skills to implement community health promotion and prevention activities and to enhance the capability and capacity of community members to support individuals with communication and swallowing needs.

Students will independently engage online learning resources while having authentic learning experiences with peers and teaching staff in weekly tutorials. In the tutorials, students engage in peer-to-peer learning through active participation in independent, small group and large group learning activities.

learning_outcomes.description: Discuss how to partner, collaborate, and advocate for communities in speech pathology practice
Identify speech pathology needs of a community
Enhance the capability and capacity of community members to support individuals with communication and swallowing needs

Explain how speech pathologists would provide service level responses for communities that are informed by the goals and needs of the community, planned and delivered using best available evidence, and guided by local context
Implement community health promotion and prevention activities to address social determinants of health inequity in speech pathology

Explore how social determinants and ways of knowing and being can support the development of culturally responsive services when working in collaboration with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities, Elders and cultural leaders
Explain how to engage in culturally responsive care when working in collaboration with culturally and linguistically diverse communities

assessment_summary.title: Group Debate: The value of investment in community-focussed activities
Needs Analysis and Elevator Pitch Assignment

assessment_details.title: Group Debate: The value of investment in community-focussed activities
assessment_details.task_description: This task will be assessed during tutorial time. It aims to develop students' skills in advocating for community-focussed activities and health promotion and prevention initiatives within speech pathology practice. It aligns with the need for speech pathologists to effectively communicate and influence decision-makers in their workplace. Part A - Prior to the tutorial in Week 4, groups will be assigned a topic and stance ('for' or 'against') related to community-focused or health promotion and prevention activities. In their group, students will participate in a structured debate with the opposing group assigned to the same topic. Part B - Following the debate, students will complete a UQ peer evaluation tool, Buddycheck, to demonstrate how each student has contributed to the debate. The task description and marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard. All presentations will be recorded for marking and moderation purposes only. These recordings will not be released to Blackboard and will be only used by the course staff. Extensions Extensions can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for an extension request via my.UQ > my.UQ Dashboard > my. Requests and provide evidence of circumstance, as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than the assessment item submission date. Further information regarding what constitutes an acceptable reason for an extension is listed on the my.UQ website: Applying for an extension - my.UQ - University of Queensland *This assessment item is a group assessment. If a student is unable to attend a tutorial due to medical or other valid reasons, the group is still expected to complete the task collectively during the tutorial and receive a group mark. The student who was absent should anticipate a similar individual task and is required to submit an extension request via My Requests before the tutorial, and also email the course coordinator - Dr Katelyn Melvin (k.melvin@uq.edu.au) in advance to arrange for the assessment to be completed individually at a later date. Use of AI Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Cultural Safety Professional Action Plan
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment has been designed to be completed by students after they engage with Module 4 of the Understanding Cultural Safety modules. Students will be provided with a cultural safety continuum reflective tool and template to develop a professional action plan. They will identify three specific actions they will take to advance their culturally safe practice, and link these actions to the stages of the cultural safety continuum (i.e., unlearning, learning, applying, reflecting, embedding). Use of AI Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The Cultural Safety Action Plan is to be submitted through TurnItIn via Blackboard. Instructions for submitting your assignment through TurnItIn can be found at https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-services/it/learnuq-blackboard-help/learnuq-assessment/turnitin-assignments/submit-your-turnitin-assignment.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Needs Analysis and Elevator Pitch Assignment
assessment_details.task_description: This assignment challenges you to critically analyse health and communication/ swallowing challenges within the Australian context, identify gaps in current approaches, and propose collaborative solutions. This task has two parts, which are submitted together. Part A - Students will be asked to respond to a contemporary issue and advocate for individuals in a community. Students will first complete a written task, which will include a needs analysis for their assigned community (20% weighting). They will discuss the role of the speech pathologist in supporting the needs of that community with reference to recent literature. Part B - Students will then submit a recorded elevator pitch (oral presentation) focusing on a solution to the contemporary issue, outlining a community proposal, and justifying why their proposed solution should be funded and implemented (20% weighting). The task description and marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard. Use of AI Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:

assessment_details.title: Written Exam
assessment_details.task_description: In this final assessment, students will complete a written exam featuring a case-based community scenario. Drawing on course content, students will apply principles of community development and engagement to outline their approach to working with the community in the scenario. The exam will also require students to reflect on the evolving role of the speech pathologist, integrating key learnings from SPCH3211. This assessment is designed to evaluate students' ability to synthesise theory and practice in context. This exam will be delivered in Inspera Assessment and will be completed on your personal laptop on campus. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 10 minutes Duration 90 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Closed book examination - no written materials permitted Exam platform Inspera Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam . Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request.
